description_long: This is a traditional stone water well with a circular base constructed from large, irregularly shaped gray stones, forming a sturdy wall. The well is topped with a wooden frame consisting of two vertical posts and a horizontal beam, all made from weathered timber. Suspended from the center of the beam is a cylindrical wooden bucket, attached by a thin rope or chain, which can be lowered into the well. The interior of the well appears open, with the bucket positioned above the opening, ready for use. The structure is robust and utilitarian, with no visible decorative elements, and is designed to provide access to groundwater in an urban or park setting. The overall appearance is rustic, emphasizing function over ornamentation.
description: A rustic stone water well with a wooden frame and suspended bucket, featuring a circular stone wall and weathered timber supports.
description_view_0: Side view showing the thick stone wall and the wooden frame's vertical post directly in front, partially obscuring the bucket.
description_view_1: Frontal view highlighting the open well, the suspended wooden bucket, and the full width of the wooden frame.
description_view_2: Opposite side view with the stone wall and a vertical wooden post in the foreground, bucket partially visible inside.
description_view_3: Alternate frontal view with the bucket clearly centered above the well opening and the wooden frame fully visible.
attribute_presence_of_pulley_or_bucket_m: A wooden bucket is suspended by a rope from the central wooden beam, serving as the water-drawing mechanism.
attribute_surrounding_protective_structu: A circular stone wall approximately 0.8 meters high surrounds the well, acting as a protective barrier.
